name: The Oracle of Delphi
description: The hallucinogenic center of the ancient world.

name: Meteora
description: Monasteries on rock pillars, once accessible only by frayed ropes.

name: Antikythera Mechanism
description: A 2,000-year-old computer demonstrates remarkable engineering and astronomical precision.

name: Sami
description: A spectacular cave and a beguiling lake.

name: The Athenian Agora
description: This ancient Greek place of assembly and marketplace is being revealed layer by layer below the modern Athens cityscape.

name: Navagio Beach
description: Hidden in a secluded Greek cove is a pristine beach marred only by the rusting smuggler's hulk stranded on its shores.

name: Lycabettus Funicular
description: The railway carts travelers to central Athens's highest peak, which offers stunning vistas of the city.

name: Anafiotika
description: The Cycladic architecture makes this Athens neighborhood feel like an island paradise hidden in the shadow of the Acropolis.

name: Hadrian's Reservoir
description: This ancient hydrological marvel is now the base of a modern outdoor cinema.

name: Tourlitis Lighthouse
description: Perched on a spindly rock spire in a Greek archipelago, this lighthouse would make a perfect wizard's tower.

name: Magic Sphere of Helios
description: This mystical orb covered in strange symbols is thought to have been used in ancient Greek magical rituals.

name: Knossos
description: A minotaur, a labyrinth, and a dubious restoration pull in visitors from around the world.

name: Mycenae
description: This legendary city has been a tourist attraction for centuries.

name: Theatre of Dionysus
description: Heralded as the world's first theater, these ruins are often overlooked as they reside in the shadows of the Acropolis.

name: Tower of the Winds
description: An ancient octagonal weather station named for the eight Greek gods of wind.

name: First Cemetery of Athens
description: The oldest cemetery of modern-day Athens blends the modern and ancient worlds.

name: Atlantis Books
description: A quirky bookstore hidden beneath a sea of whitewashed Greek villas.

name: The Olive Tree of the Acropolis
description: Legend says this sacred tree was a gift from goddess Athena.

name: Prehistoric Town of Akrotiri
description: A Bronze age settlement on the Greek island of Santorini might have been the inspiration for Plato's Atlantis.

name: Dikteon Cave
description: According to myth, Zeus himself was hidden in this cave to escape being eaten by his dad.

name: Dromeas (The Runner)
description: A sharp sculpture made of thousands of pieces of jagged glass.

name: The Temple of Dionysos
description: Giant marble phalluses on the sacred island of antiquity.

name: Roman Tomb in Zara
description: The ruins of an ancient Roman tomb hidden in the basement of a trendy clothing store.

name: Mystras
description: The ruins of an abandoned medieval city where the last Byzantine emperor was crowned.

name: Agia Dynamis
description: This small 16th-century church in Athens is almost entirely surrounded by a modern building.

name: Labyrinthos Caves
description: Cretean quarry suggests real-life site for ancient Greek myth of the man-eating Minotaur in his labyrinth.

name: Kastania Cave
description: This remarkable cave was discovered after a shepherd watched bees enter and exit through a small fissure.

name: Old Madrasa of Athens
description: The ruined doorway is a portal to the building's turbulent and torturous past.

name: Terrace of the Lions
description: Replicas of Apollo's leonine stone guard cats still line a sacred row in Greece.

name: Styx Waterfall
description: This 200-meter-high waterfall marks the site of Achilles’ immortal baptism at the mouth of the River Styx.

name: Wax Museum of Andreas Syggros
description: A grotesque collection of wax limbs depicting the symptoms of venereal disease.

name: Panathenaic Stadium Passage
description: Gladiators once walked this ancient, subterranean passage.

name: Goulandris Museum of Cycladic Art
description: Large collection from the 3rd millennium BCE.

name: Thessaloniki Train Cemetery
description: Thousands of rusting train cars have been slowly falling apart on the edge of Greece's second largest city.

name: Pyrgi
description: This medieval "painted village" is famous for its geometric facades.

name: Cape Matapan Caves
description: A cave network located at the southernmost tip of mainland Greece was once believed to be an entrance to Hades.

name: Diporto
description: With no menu and no sign, this 130-year-old taverna doles out affordable home cooking.

name: Davelis Cave on Pendeli Mountain
description: Hideout of thieves, monks, and goat-footed gods.

name: Syntagma Square Metro Station
description: Where commuters walk through remnants of an ancient Greek necropolis.

name: Volcanic Rocks of Lemnos
description: An alien landscape on the banks of the Aegean Sea.

name: Olive Tree of Vouves
description: The oldest olive tree in Greece has been bearing fruit for at least 2,000 years.

name: Dimitrios
description: This wrecked ghost ship has been rusting on a Greek beach since the 1980s.

name: Necromanteion of Ephyra
description: Ancient Greek oracle of the dead.

name: Drakolimni
description: Local lore says two enemy dragons live within these breathtaking mountain lakes.

name: Elafonisi (Deer Island)
description: A small island with shimmering pink sands, home to loggerhead turtles and rare plants.

name: Sculpted Face on Shore
description: Poseidon? No one seems to know.

name: Mount Athos
description: An autonomous republic of Orthodox monks.

name: Konitsa Bridge
description: One of the largest single-span stone-arch bridges in the Balkans looks like something straight out of a fairytale.

name: Pythagoras Cave
description: Cave on extinct volcano, once home to famous mathematician Pythagoras.

name: The Ruins of Rentina
description: These ruins in Volvi were once at the intersection of the Eastern and Western Worlds.

name: Pasha Gardens
description: A little-known park with unusual, half-ruined structures and a mystical energy.

name: Nicopolis
description: The ruins of this monumental city founded by the first Roman Emperor are strangely overlooked by most travelers.

name: Park of Souls
description: After a catastrophic forest fire, a Greek artist transformed scorched tree trunks outside an old sanatorium into this haunting sculpture garden.

name: Church of Agios Dimitrios Loumbardiaris (Saint Demetrius the Bombardier)
description: A Byzantine-era church hidden in a wooded area below the Acropolis.

name: Little Metropolis Church
description: This gem is often wrongly overshadowed by the larger, newer Metropolis church in Athens.

name: Chios Rocket War
description: Two churches shoot bottle rockets at each others' bell towers in an unusual Easter celebration.

name: Holy Kipinas Monastery
description: This 13th-century monastery chiseled into the side of a mountain is equipped with a bridge that can be lifted to protect against raids.

name: Thessaloniki Brothel
description: A two thousand year old brothel uncovered in Northern Greece.

name: Spinalonga Island
description: This abandoned Greek island was the last active leper colony in Europe.

name: Petraki Monastery
description: The oldest standing Byzantine church in Athens is tucked within a monastery in the middle of an upscale shopping district.

name: Botanical Museum of the National Garden
description: This small museum once served as a royal hunting pavilion for King Otto Friedrich Ludwig.

name: Fallen Aviators Monument
description: This striking sculpture of Icarus honors Greece's fallen flyers.

name: Caves of Filopappou Hill
description: These caves secretly held national treasures during World War II.

name: Kafeneio Oraia Ellas
description: Luminaries of all stripes have sipped strong Greek coffee at this cafe since 1839.

name: Valley of the Butterflies
description: Swarms of dainty flying insects swarm into the Petaloudes Valley to get busy.

name: Parilissia Iera
description: An often overlooked ancient site in downtown Athens with substantial history (and nature).

name: Phaistos Disc
description: This famous archeological mystery may be an ancient example of movable type.

name: Heptapyrgion Fortress
description: An abandoned Byzantine fortress and notorious prison.

name: Symi Harbour
description: This secret coastal village is studded with pastel-colored houses all piled on top of one another.

name: St. Theodora's Chapel
description: Seventeen oak trees sprout from the roof and walls of this tiny chapel without any visual evidence of roots.

name: Bee Museum of Rhodes
description: Learn about the sweet, storied legacy of beekeeping in ancient Greece.

name: Zappeion
description: A man's head is enshrined beneath a statue near the first building constructed for the modern Olympic Games.

name: Bourtzi
description: This 15th-century island fortress later served as home to the executioners no one wanted to live near.

name: Plutonian Cave of Eleusis
description: According to legend, this cavern holds the passage that Persephone used to travel to and from the underworld.

name: Numismatic Museum of Athens
description: One of the greatest collections of coins in the world is housed in the former mansion of an archeological pioneer.

name: Gestapo Interrogation Memorial
description: The site where hundreds of Greeks were tortured by the Nazi secret police is now a cosmetics store.

name: Abandoned Bogomils Graveyard
description: This field of half-buried stone crosses was the burial place of a sect of heretical gnostics.

name: Perithia at Corfu
description: The ghost town where the villagers refuse to leave.

name: Samothrace Sanctuary of the Great Gods
description: Temple complex that once housed the famous statue of Nike.

name: Areopolis
description: This town named for the ancient God of War is home to fewer than a thousand people, but it's the birthplace of the Greek Revolution.

name: The Gennadius Library
description: A sprawling collection of books and manuscripts about ancient Greece that would have put their famed libraries to shame.

name: Hermitages of Karoulia
description: The hermits of Karoulia protect their asocial lifestyles by living on the vertical face of a treacherous precipice.

name: Korai 4
description: Scars of this building's past life as a violent prison still cover its walls.

name: Old Navarino Castle
description: Ruins of a 13th-century fortress perched above a breathtaking Mediterranean bay.

name: Stani
description: Savor sheep milk yogurt at the last of Athens' dairy bars.

name: Temple of Artemis Brauronia
description: This seaside sanctuary was once one of the most important religious sites for women in ancient Greece.

name: Athens Olympic Sports Complex
description: What once sat in the world’s spotlight is today a largely disused ghost complex.

name: Lovokomeio Leper Colony
description: An ambiguous ambience emanates from this centuries old leprosy quarantine.

name: Dion Archaeological Park
description: This open air archeological site houses almost as much wildlife as it does classical religious artifacts.

name: Poulopoulos Hat Factory
description: This graffitied 19th-century building was a prototype of European industrial architecture.

name: Petrified Forest of Lesbos
description: The rocky remains of an ancient Greek forest.

name: Archaeological Site Inside Monastiraki Metro Station
description: View the ancient remains of buildings and the ancient "River of Hades" before hopping on a train.

name: Fethiye Mosque
description: This historic structure has been used as a mosque, a barracks, a prison, a school, and a bakery.

name: The Athens Polytechnic Monument
description: A crushed gate and giant bronze head serve as a reminder of Greece's fight for freedom.

name: Ritual of Periarosis Column
description: According to legend, this ancient column contains healing properties.

name: Archeological Exhibit at Athens International Airport
description: These artifacts were discovered during the construction of the airport, with some dating back to the Neolithic period.

name: Stoa Emporon
description: This abandoned shopping arcade has new life as an art installation.

name: Paralia Perissa
description: This black sand beach was crafted from volcanic explosions including the historic Minoan eruption.

name: Krinos
description: This century-old, family-run bakery specializes in loukoumades, a Greek doughnut once served to ancient Olympians.

name: The Neith of Spetses
description: Overlooking the harbor is a beautiful neglected mansion guarded by two Egyptian sphinxes.

name: Frangokastello Castle
description: Every spring, locals see the shadowy figures of a ghost army of slain Greek soldiers descend upon this ruined fort.

name: Divani Palace Acropolis Fortification Wall
description: A preserved portion of an ancient Athens barrier is displayed in a hotel basement.

name: Ariston
description: The Lobotesis family has been baking some of the best savory pies in Athens since 1910.

name: St. Catherine's Greek Orthodox Church
description: A Byzantine church containing the holy remnants of three saints sits atop the site of an ancient temple.

name: Holy Church of the Archangels
description: This small chapel holds the mummified heart of one of the heroes of the Greek Revolution.

name: Entomological Museum of Volos
description: This little-known, 35,000-specimen collection in Greece is a lepidopterist's dream.

name: Cafe Avissinia
description: Catch raucous live performances of traditional tunes at this old-school taverna.

name: To Steki Tou Ilia
description: Tear apart fatty, gently charred lamb chops with your bare hands.

name: Tatoi Palace
description: The abandoned summer estate of the former Greek Royal Family.

name: Fatsio
description: Since 1969, this restaurant has served up dishes typical of Istanbul's Greek diaspora.

name: Vatheia
description: The historic tower houses of this nearly abandoned Greek town have been compared to the spikes on an iguana's back.

name: Windmills of Lasithi Plateau
description: This little Greek plateau is home to thousands of rustic, white-sailed windmills.

name: Kouros of Apollonas
description: This ancient statue of Dionysus lies unfinished and abandoned in one of Greece's oldest quarries.

name: Epta Piges (Seven Springs)
description: A dark, narrow tunnel that leads to a charming lake and waterfall.

name: The British Cemetery
description: The Greek island's old British cemetery feels like a forgotten garden.

name: Sirocco
description: This "volcanic all-day restaurant" is right on the beach.

name: Lemnos Sand Dunes
description: A miniature desert appears out of nowhere on the lush isle of Lemnos, like something out of a Greek myth.

name: Catacombs of Milos
description: An intricate network of underground tombs carved into volcanic rock.

name: Panta Vrechei Canyon
description: A series of waterfalls create the illusion of perpetual rainfall in this hidden gorge.

name: St. John Around the Column
description: There are many Byzantine-era churches still standing in Athens, but this is the only one with a 2,500-year-old column rising through its roof.

name: Church of Agia Kyriaki
description: A tiny Greek Orthodox church from the Ottoman era hides on a busy city street.

name: Choragic Monument of Lysicrates
description: The only surviving choragic monument is a trophy awarded in 334 BC for the winning dramatic chorus.

name: Street of the Knights
description: One of the best-preserved medieval streets in Europe.

name: Eleni Marneri Galerie Late Antiquity Urban Villa
description: The ruins of an ancient mansion sits in front of and underneath a contemporary art gallery.

name: Fairytale Castle of Agrili
description: A magical, if a bit tacky, 1960s castle conjures up medieval fairytales and Greek mythology.

name: Tholos Tomb of Tiryns
description: Step inside an ancient beehive tomb built for a mysterious cult hero.

name: Tholos Tomb of Menidi
description: This beehive tomb built for royalty sat hidden for over 3,000 years.

name: Xigia Sulphur Caves
description: These sea caves are the source of Xigia Beach's distinct aroma.

name: Temple of Artemis Agrotera
description: The only ancient site that remains on the south bank of the sacred Ilissos river.

name: Komboloi Museum (Greek Worry Bead Museum)
description: The secular incarnation of a religious meditation.

name: Nea Moni
description: This 11th-century Greek monastery has renowned mosaics and relics of a brutal past.

name: Tomato Industrial Museum
description: A former factory serves as a shrine to the industry that sustained Santorini for decades.

name: Lion of Kea
description: This smily stone feline is meant to represent a mythical lion that almost destroyed the island of Kea.

name: Temple of Apollo Zoster
description: These ruins of a sacred Ancient Greek temple stand next to a luxurious modern resort.

name: Ruins at Coco Mat Athens B.C. Hotel
description: When construction began on this hotel, developers were surprised to find the ruins of an ancient civilization.

name: Choregic Monument of Thrasyllos (Panagia Speliotissa)
description: An ancient Greek monument and a Byzantine church coexist inside this cave.

name: Kalograion Monastery Silk Weaving
description: The nuns here cultivate silk to weave some of the finest scarves in the world.

name: Tzistarakis Mosque
description: Legend says it was constructed with the destroyed pillar of an ancient Greek Temple.

name: Proteichisma on Erysichthonos Street
description: The ruins of this ancient fortification wall sit in the middle of a quiet, residential intersection.

name: Klafthmonos Square Parking Structure Fortification Walls
description: Preserved portions of Athens's ancient defenses are on display in a subterranean parking garage.

name: Kotsanas Museum of Ancient Greek Technology
description: Get hands on with the technological marvels of the Classical world.

name: Lion of Bavaria
description: This slumbering beast honors the Bavarian soldiers who died in Greece during a 19th-century typhoid outbreak.

name: Yeni Camii
description: This architecturally eclectic early-20th century mosque was built as a place of worship for the Islamic followers of a Jewish messiah.

name: Arkadiko Bridge
description: Built by the Ancient Greeks, this chariot bridge is still in use today.

name: Church of Agios Spyridon
description: The historic building has preserved human organs, unusual hexagrams, and scars from a political assassination.

name: Prosfygika
description: This once architecturally advanced housing complex is now a haven for squatters that still bears the scars of war.

name: Daphni Monastery
description: This lesser-visited World Heritage site in Athens boasts an impressive ensemble of medieval gold mosaics.

name: Beauties & Hardships
description: This quaint farm in the Peloponnese countryside hides an astonishing cache of antique curiosities.

name: Tourlida Islet
description: An islet filled with wooden houses where the open sea meets a lagoon.

name: House of Proclus
description: Underneath one of the region's most historic roads lies the home of Plato's successor.

name: Tower of Markellos
description: This pink watchtower served as the seat of the Greek government after independence from the Turks.

name: Church of Panagia Mesosporitissa
description: Only open for two days a year, this church links ancient Greek and modern Christian celebrations.

name: Aliki Ancient Marble Quarry
description: The partially submerged ruins of a white marble quarry used in ancient Greece for over 1,200 years.

name: Valerian Wall at Niche Hotel
description: The ruins of an ancient fortification wall sit in front of and underneath this boutique hotel.

name: Ancient Ruins on Selley Street
description: These ancient ruins hide in plain sight next to a dessert shop.

name: Church of Agioi Pantes
description: A staircase made of gravestones leads up to this church built into a giant rock.

name: Kalavryta Sacrifice Memorial
description: A solemn reminder of the infamous World War II war crime that massacred almost all the men in a small Greek town.

name: Tsambika Monastery
description: Scores of wax baby figurines add an unusual mood to this beautiful church on the Greek coast.

name: Church of Agios Sostis
description: This Byzantine Church was moved from Paris to Athens block by block.

name: First Ancient Theatre of Larissa
description: Dedicated to the god Dionysus, this theatre was used for both musical and theatrical performances.

state: Decentralized Administration of Peloponnese, Western Greece and the Ionian
name: Strofades Monastery
description: A lonely medieval monastery on an uninhabited island.

name: Church of Agia Fotini Mantineias
description: This church combines Christian, Byzantine, and ancient Greek architectural elements.

name: Castle of Agia Mavra (Santa Maura)
description: A seaside castle complex that stood through centuries of conquests.

name: Alykes Salt Flats
description: These abandoned salt flats are now a haven for wildlife.

name: Ottoman Fountains of Nafplio
description: Public water fountains built during the Ottoman Era still stand throughout this seaside town.

name: Austrian Archaeological Institute at Athens
description: A historic building houses one of the most important archaeological institutes in Europe.

name: 'The Umbrellas'
description: One of the most photographed locations in the entire city.

name: Church of Agioi Apostoloi (Church of the Holy Apostles)
description: The Greek War of Independence was declared at this medieval Byzantine sanctuary.

name: Stones of Frederica
description: Elaborate stone houses constructed from the ruins of war.

name: Ziller-Loverdos Mansion
description: One of the best collections of Byzantine art housed in a magnificent, 19th-century neoclassical home.

name: The Aqueduct of Kavala
description: This ancient structure appears Roman, but was constructed during the 16th century and was utilized until 1911.

name: Lakki Cinema
description: This Greek cinema was architecturally inspired by Mussolini's vision of a fascist Mediterranean utopia.

name: Amalias 42
description: This building displays the scars of the “Dekemvriana,” a dark time in modern Greek history.

name: Isthmia Prime Hotel
description: A closer look at this somewhat faded hotel by the Corinth Canal reveals the architectural undertones of a genius.

name: Monastery Penteli
description: The heads and relics of 179 monks slaughtered by pirates are on display within this Ceonobic Monastery.

name: Pyrgos Vasilissis (Queens Tower)
description: This fairytale-looking castle is a wonderful example of Greek neo-Gothic architecture

name: Church of Agia Pareskevi
description: This Ottoman-era Greek Orthodox Church was built to honor Agia Pareskevi, a Christian saint.

name: Church of the Virgin Mary
description: This sixth-century church housed an icon with eyes so beautiful legend has it they inspired the name of a Greek city.

name: Magoula Cemetery
description: Modernist design and funerary customs meet Spartan traditions in this one-of-a-kind graveyard.

name: Vouleftikon (People's Assembly)
description: This 18th-century former mosque held the first Hellenic Parliament.

name: Chania Lighthouse
description: This stone tower was built to protect Crete from incoming pirates.

name: The Blue Polykatoikia
description: An important example of architectural history slowly decays in the city center.

name: Monastery of Panagia Faneromeni
description: After being destroyed by two separate fires, this monastery is now home to a small zoo and relics from dozens of saints.

name: Church of Agios Demetrios of Diakos
description: This Ottoman-era church is hidden amid a busy tourist area.

name: Museum of Greek Folk Musical Instruments
description: Dating from the 18th century to today, this museum houses a collection of over 1,200 musical instruments.

name: Pyramid on Mount Taygetos
description: The top of the holy mountain for the ancient Spartans looks like a pyramid—is it man-made or a natural phenomenon?

name: Asyrmatos Building
description: A unique housing complex born from the aftermath of the Greek Civil War.

name: Vrykolakonisia (Vampire Islands)
description: The alleged vampires after which these islets are named may have actually been plague victims.

name: Catholic Church of Agios Georgios
description: On top of a hill, this Catholic church overlooks the most Catholic island in Greece.

name: The Lion of Chaeronea
description: Τhis feline monument commemorates fallen soldiers on a field that saw many, many battles.

name: T. H. White’s Grave
description: The burial place of one the greatest Arthurian authors, with the sword in the (grave)stone.

name: Church of the Holy Apostle Philip at Vlassarous
description: This church was constructed on the site where St. Philip the Apostle preached to the ancient Athenians.

name: Kostis Palamas House
description: The home of Greece’s national poet sits crumbling in the middle of a popular tourist district.

name: Basilica of Agios Nikolaos
description: This church is dedicated to the patron saint of seafarers.

name: Weiler Building
description: A former military hospital is now used to study and preserve artifacts of the Acropolis.

name: Church of St. Spyridon
description: This Greek Orthodox church houses the mummified body of St. Spyridon.

name: Suda Bay War Cemetery
description: 1,500 Commonwealth servicemen of World War II are buried or commemorated in this cemetery.

name: Abandoned Tropo Station of Lefkada
description: The remains of a military communications outpost still stand on this Greek island.

name: Agios Isidoros
description: A stunning little chapel built on a rock in the Mediterranean Sea on the Greek island of Leros.

name: The Well at Meligalas
description: A somber memorial at the site where hundreds of prisoners and civilians were executed in the aftermath of World War II.

name: Holy Church of the Dormition of the Virgin Mary
description: Believed to be the only Orthodox church in the world that features a free-standing pulpit.

name: Nymfopetra
description: Legends hold that these unusual rock formations were once a bridal party, or perhaps a group of hunters.

name: Aiolou Street Excavation Site
description: An ancient site that has revealed incredible artifacts including portions of the Themistoclean Wall.

name: Caves of Diros
description: This cave complex might provide a link between the Neolithic and the Classical Greek period.

name: Museum of Cycladic Folklore
description: This old museum contains dozens of detailed miniature ships and buildings.

name: Philopappou Hill Pathways
description: Rest stops along this pathway offer some of the best views of the Acropolis of Athens.

name: War Museum Askifou
description: This collection of artifacts represents one family's mission to record the horrors of World War II inflicted on their hometown.

name: Garden of the Presidential Mansion
description: A former royal garden with lush surroundings in the heart of the city.

name: The House of Stone Creations
description: Nestled in the hills of Crete, a local artist has created a wonderland of stone and terracotta sculptures.

name: Profitis Ilias Monastery
description: A hilltop monastery on the Greek island of Hydra.

name: ZEP Kissos
description: A settlement that was stopped in the middle of construction, leaving behind paved roads, curbs, light poles, a stormwater drainage system, and even a basketball court, but no houses.

name: Oracle of Trophonius
description: This sanctuary became synonymous with fear and terror in the ancient world.

name: Holy Church of Saint Symeon of the Anapheans
description: This post-Ottoman era church in Athens was built by locals to serve the residents of Anafiotica

name: World's Largest Greek Flag
description: Flying high over the small island of Zákynthos is a giant tribute to the mainland.

name: Saint Nikolaos of Vouliagmeni
description: Past the megayachts in the hyper-modern marina of Vouliagmeni is a small, well-hidden chapel adorned with pebbles and maritime imagery.

name: Holy Church of Saint Andrew
description: A martyred saint built a nunnery and school for girls here known as the "Parthenon."

name: Corfu Museum of Asian Art
description: A spectacular Neoclassical palace is home more than 15,000 artifacts and works of art.

name: Kaisariani Shooting Range
description: A memorial dedicated to a tragic event during the Axis occupation of Greece.

name: Store for Unfinished Foreign Territory
description: This building served as the first customs house in modern Greece.

name: The Armansperg Residence
description: This building was once the most magnificent mansion in Nafplio.

name: Mill of the Elves (Matsopoulos Mill)
description: This former mill comes to life during the Christmas season.

name: Holy Church of Panagia Theoskepasti
description: A stunning church overlooking the water that you will have all to yourself.

name: Mycenaean Cemetery of Voudeni
description: Overlooked archeological site close to the city of Patras.

name: Banknote Museum of the Ionian Bank
description: The history of Greece is illustrated through banknotes.

name: Xanthi Clock Tower
description: This tower is all that remains of the mosque that once stood in the center of the city, before it was destroyed by fire in 1941.

name: Church of Saint Nicholas (Agios Nikolaos Church)
description: A mystery of a buried artist and a 14th-century church.

name: Alyko Hotel Ruins
description: Graffiti-covered ruins of a luxurious resort sit among enchanting beaches.

name: Rock of Kalikatsou
description: A Christian pilgrimage site that may have also been a temple for the Greek goddess of love.

name: Meton's Observatory
description: The site where the summer solstice helped ancient Greeks sync the lunar and solar calendars.

name: Tower of Sir Richard Church / House of George Finlay
description: The second oldest surviving house in Athens was owned by an Irish General.

name: Eleousa (Campochiaro)
description: Former Italian settlement on the isle of Rhodes was a WWII command center before being abandoned.

name: The Athens University History Museum (Kleanthis House)
description: The Acropolis house that became Modern Greece’s first university.

name: Pallis Building
description: A pre-war mansion that survived the modernization of Athens.

name: Kifissia Mosque Ruins
description: The only Ottoman Mosque in Attica outside central Athens.

name: Pittaki Street
description: 150 or so scrounged-up lamps, chandeliers, and lanterns light up this alley in central Athens.

name: Bensousan Han
description: An arts and culture melting pot inside an old roadside inn from the Ottoman era.

name: Kanavourgio
description: Once the largest rope factory in Greece, this abandoned plant now stands like a ghost of industry past.
